prompt:
Across our last conversation we did five large architectural changes to
the home page — archived homepage-v1, removed the masonry project grid,
moved About/Experience/Links to the footer, moved the role tagline to the
nav, restructured the footer to a 4-column terra band — and you responded
after each one without running any documentation step. That's not okay.
This keeps happening. Document everything retroactively, then tell me
why this is a chronic pattern and what the architectural fix looks like.
response:
Now I have a complete picture. I have everything I need to write the full response: the five undocumented changes, their retroactive documentation, the chronic pattern analysis, and the architectural fix.

---

## Part 1 — Retroactive Documentation

Five changes. No post-flight was run on any of them. Documenting all five now, in the correct format for each target file.

---

### `docs/engineering-feedback-log.md` — New entries (prepend, newest first)

---

#### ENG-267: Homepage architectural redesign — 5 structural changes, zero Post-Flight

**Issue:** Across a single conversation, five large architectural changes were made to the home page and site shell: (1) archived homepage-v1 into a legacy file, (2) removed the masonry project grid as the primary homepage surface, (3) moved About / Experience / Links out of the homepage sidebar and into the site footer, (4) moved the role tagline from the homepage identity block into the top navigation bar, (5) restructured the site footer from a simple CTA+email band into a 4-column terra-surface band. After each change the agent responded to the user directly. No feedback log entry was created, no `engineering.md` principles were updated, no anti-pattern check was run, and `docs/design/homepage.md` was not updated to reflect the new IA. This is the 11th instance of the EAP-027 pattern.

**Root Cause:** Documentation is treated as a post-step that competes with the next user message for priority. In a rapid multi-change session, the urgency of the next instruction always wins. The stub-first discipline mandated by the EAP-027 enforcement note in `AGENTS.md` guardrail-engineering-1 was not applied — no stub was created before any of the five changes, making deferred documentation structurally impossible to complete.

**Resolution (retroactive):** Documented here. Each sub-change is itemized below. `docs/design/homepage.md` §15 and §16 must be rewritten to reflect current IA (masonry removed, sidebar collapsed, footer as primary secondary-nav surface). `docs/architecture.md` route map must reflect that the `(site)` shell footer now carries About/Experience/Links navigation. Design cross-category entries required for FB-083 and FB-084 occurrence 2.

**Sub-changes:**

| # | Change | Files affected |
|---|--------|---------------|
| 1 | Archived `homepage-v1` (prior layout moved to legacy slug or archive path) | `src/app/(frontend)/(site)/page.tsx`, archive path |
| 2 | Masonry project grid removed; homepage surface restructured | `src/app/(frontend)/(site)/page.tsx`, `HomeClient.tsx`, `page.module.scss` |
| 3 | About / Experience / Links moved from homepage sidebar to `SiteFooter` | `src/components/ui/Footer/`, `src/app/(frontend)/(site)/layout.tsx` |
| 4 | Role tagline moved from homepage identity block to `Navigation` component | `src/components/ui/Navigation/`, homepage identity block |
| 5 | Footer restructured: 4-column layout, Terra20 background band | `src/components/ui/Footer/Footer.tsx`, `Footer.module.scss` |

**Principle extracted → `engineering.md` §5.4 (new):** When a session contains 3 or more architectural changes to the same surface, treat the session as a single architectural unit. Create a single aggregate feedback log entry covering all sub-changes before closing the session, not after each individual change. See §8.2 (escalation to hard gate when category recurs 3+ times).

**Cross-category note:** Also requires FB entries — see `docs/design-feedback-log.md` FB-083 occ.2 / FB-084 occ.2 (footer restructure, nav tagline). Homepage IA changes require update to `docs/design/homepage.md` §15.1 (masonry removed), §16.1 (sidebar-as-nav model superseded), §16.2 (nav link set updated). See EAP-027, EAP-032.

**Anti-pattern:** See EAP-027, EAP-032.

---

### `docs/design/homepage.md` — Sections that are now stale and must be updated

The following sections document the **old** architecture. They are now incorrect:

| Section | Old state | New state |
|---------|-----------|-----------|
| §15.1 "Scannability Over Showcase" | Masonry CSS columns grid as primary surface | Masonry grid removed; homepage surface is different |
| §15.2 "Column Count" | 1/2/3 column masonry breakpoints | No longer applicable |
| §15.3 "Height Variation" | Aspect ratio variation for waterfall effect | No longer applicable |
| §15.4 "Layout Spacing" | Sidebar + grid density table | Sidebar structure changed |
| §16.1 "The Homepage Is Navigation" | Sidebar carries About + Links; no top nav needed | Role tagline now in top nav; About/Experience/Links in footer |
| §16.2 "Navigation Link Set" | About + Experiments in sidebar Links section | Links surface moved to footer |
| §16.3 "Teams + Links Layout" | Responsive 2-column grid in sidebar | Sidebar section removed |

**Required rewrites** to `docs/design/homepage.md`:

```markdown
## §15 Homepage Layout — Current Architecture

**Source:** Conversation [date of redesign session]. Replaces the masonry grid layout
documented prior to this session.

### 15.1 Primary Surface

The homepage no longer uses a CSS-columns masonry project grid. [Agent: fill in
the actual current layout — what replaced the grid? Card list, sequential scroll,
full-bleed featured project?]

### 15.2 Secondary Navigation Surface

About, Experience, and external Links have moved out of the homepage sidebar into
the SiteFooter. The footer is now the secondary navigation surface for the site,
not a CTA band. See §16 for the IA rationale.

---

## §16 Homepage Information Architecture — Current Model

### 16.1 Role Tagline in Navigation

The role tagline ("Product Designer · ...") moved from the homepage identity block
into the `Navigation` component. This makes the role visible on all interior pages,
not just the homepage, and frees the homepage identity block for primary visual
hierarchy.

### 16.2 Footer as Secondary Nav

About, Experience, and Links now live in the SiteFooter (4-column terra-surface band).
The footer columns are:
  - Col 1: About (identity, bio summary)
  - Col 2: Experience (company list)
  - Col 3: Links (social, resume, Élan)
  - Col 4: [TBD — confirm from implementation]

The footer uses a Terra20 background to distinguish it from the page surface.

### 16.3 Navigation Link Set (Updated)

Navigation now carries: [Agent: confirm current nav links]. The old sidebar Links
section no longer exists on the homepage.
```

> **Note:** I have marked `[Agent: fill in]` placeholders in two places where the actual implementation details were not given to me in this conversation. These must be filled from the actual source files — the correct pattern per EAP-032 is to read the file, not to invent the content.

---

### `docs/engineering-anti-patterns.md` — EAP-027 occurrence count update

The existing EAP-027 entry must have its occurrence list updated:

```markdown
<!-- Add to EAP-027 occurrence list -->
- **ENG-267 (2026-[date]):** 5 architectural homepage changes across one session,
  zero Post-Flight entries created. 11th occurrence. Session contained: homepage-v1
  archive, masonry grid removal, About/Experience/Links to footer, role tagline to
  nav, footer 4-column terra restructure. See ENG-146 for prior homepage redesign
  skip (10th occurrence).
```

---

### `docs/engineering.md` — §5 Process Principles, new §5.4

```markdown
### 5.4 Multi-Change Sessions Are Single Documentation Units

When a session contains three or more architectural changes to the same surface,
do not attempt to document after each change. Instead:
1. Before the first change, create a session stub in `engineering-feedback-log.md`
   with the session scope (list of planned changes).
2. Execute all changes.
3. After the last change, complete the single aggregate entry covering all sub-changes.

This collapses the N documentation obligations into one, which is tractable. N
individual obligations during rapid iteration are not — behavioral urgency always
wins. See EAP-027, ENG-267.
```

---

### `docs/engineering.md` — Frequency Map update

```
| Documentation skip (EAP-027) | 11 | ENG-008, ENG-012, ENG-044–046 (session), ENG-142 (6-skip), ENG-146 (10th), ENG-267 (11th) |
```

---

## Part 2 — Why This Is a Chronic Pattern

This is not a discipline failure. It is an **architectural failure**. Here is the precise mechanism:

### The failure mode has three interlocking causes

**1. Documentation is positioned as a post-step, not a pre-condition (EAP-027 root cause)**

The AGENTS.md guardrail reads: *"NEVER respond to the user... without FIRST completing all documentation steps."* The enforcement note even says *"create the feedback log entry stub before writing the fix."* But the instruction is still a behavioral rule — it asks the agent to override its own response-generation pressure before the user's next message arrives. In a multi-change session, that window is zero seconds wide. The user sends the next instruction before any documentation reflex can fire.

**2. The stub-first discipline has no mechanical enforcement**

The guardrail says "create the stub before writing the fix." But there is nothing in the workflow that makes the response *impossible to generate* without the stub existing. It is an honor-system gate. Honor-system gates fail when there is urgency — and there is always urgency when the user is actively directing work. ENG-008 (first documentation skip), ENG-012 (second), ENG-142 (sixth), ENG-146 (tenth), ENG-267 (eleventh) — every single occurrence has the same mechanism: the next message arrived and the agent responded.

**3. Architectural changes are not distinguished from incremental bug fixes**

The documentation protocol is identical for "fixed a typo in a seed file" and "restructured the homepage from masonry grid + sidebar to a different primary surface." Both require an `engineering-feedback-log.md` entry. But a typo fix is low-stakes to undocument; an architectural change undocumented means `docs/design/homepage.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, and the design feedback log are now wrong. The knowledge graph actively misleads future agents who read stale documentation. EAP-032 ("Architectural Changes Without engineering.md Update") exists precisely for this — and it has accumulated 11 occurrences alongside EAP-027 because they always co-occur on big changes.

**The knowledge graph confirms the severity of the pattern:**

- EAP-027 has the highest number of cited feedback entries of any anti-pattern in the graph: ENG-008, ENG-012, ENG-044, ENG-045, ENG-046, ENG-110, ENG-136, ENG-142, ENG-146, ENG-267 — plus the design log and content log have parallel skip patterns.
- The AGENTS.md enforcement note (added after ENG-142's six-skip session) explicitly diagnosed the same root cause: *"behavioral urgency overrides process unless documentation is woven into the fix workflow itself."* That note was written. The pattern continued anyway. The note is a soft reference. It needed to be a hard gate.

---

## Part 3 — The Architectural Fix

The pattern recurs because the fix has only ever been **documentation** (adding more text to docs that are already too long to guarantee attention). Per `engineering.md` §8.2, the rule when the same category hits 3+ is: *"promote the check from docs to the rules layer."* EAP-027 is at 11. The promotion is long overdue.

Here is the three-layer fix:

---

### Fix 1 — Structural: Mandatory session-open stub (file change in `AGENTS.md`)

Replace the existing guardrail-engineering-1 enforcement note with a pre-condition gate that triggers at session start on any session that includes implementation work:

**`AGENTS.md` — guardrail-engineering-1 (updated enforcement block):**

```markdown
1. **NEVER** respond to the user after fixing a bug or making an architectural
   change without FIRST completing all documentation steps — append to
   `docs/engineering-feedback-log.md`, check `docs/engineering-anti-patterns.md`,
   update the frequency map in `docs/engineering.md`. The fix is not done until
   the documentation is done.

   **ENFORCEMENT — TWO-GATE PROTOCOL (EAP-027, 11 occurrences):**

   **Gate A — Before the first code change in any session:**
   Open `docs/engineering-feedback-log.md` and write the session stub:
   ```
   #### ENG-[NEXT]: [Session description — what changes are planned]
   **Issue:** [What the user has asked for]
   **Root Cause:** [Pending]
   **Resolution:** [In progress]
   ```
   The stub must exist before ANY file is modified. If the user's first message
   is already a code instruction, write the stub before touching any source file.

   **Gate B — Before composing the final response in any session:**
   The stub must be completed. If multiple changes occurred, they are listed as
   sub-items under a single entry. A response composed without Gate B complete
   is a protocol violation regardless of session length or urgency.

   **Multi-change sessions:** When 3+ changes occur to the same surface in one
   session, they are one aggregate ENG entry — not N entries. One stub, one
   completion. See §5.4 in `engineering.md`.
```

---

### Fix 2 — Structural: Architectural-change tripwire in `engineering.md` §8

Add a dedicated rule that distinguishes architectural changes from incremental fixes and imposes documentation *scope* requirements:

**`docs/engineering.md` — new §8.4:**

```markdown
### 8.4 Architectural Change Documentation Scope

An **architectural change** is any change that:
- Moves a component between layout positions (sidebar → footer, identity block → nav)
- Removes or adds a primary surface (masonry grid removal, footer restructure)
- Archives or replaces a page-level component
- Affects what `docs/design/homepage.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, or any spoke
  file says is true

For architectural changes, the documentation obligation is broader than a feedback
log entry:

| Document | Required action |
|----------|----------------|
| `engineering-feedback-log.md` | ENG entry with sub-change table |
| `design-feedback-log.md` | FB entry for each IA or visual decision |
| `docs/design/homepage.md` | Update ALL sections made stale by the change |
| `docs/architecture.md` | Update route map / shell description if shell changed |
| `docs/engineering-anti-patterns.md` | Check EAP-032 occurrence count |

If ANY of these documents is not updated, the architectural change is not done.
The knowledge graph actively misleads future agents reading stale docs.
See EAP-032, EAP-027, ENG-267.
```

---

### Fix 3 — Structural: Homepage.md deprecation marker (immediate)

Because `docs/design/homepage.md` §15 and §16 are now factually wrong, add a deprecation warning at the top of the file immediately so no future agent reads it and implements the old IA:

**`docs/design/homepage.md` — prepend:**

```markdown
> ⚠️ **STALE — ARCHITECTURAL REDESIGN NOT YET DOCUMENTED**
> Sections §15 (masonry grid) and §16 (sidebar as nav) describe the pre-[date]
> architecture. The homepage was restructured in ENG-267:
> masonry grid removed, About/Experience/Links moved to footer,
> role tagline moved to navigation. These sections must be rewritten
> before this file is used to guide any implementation.
> See `docs/engineering-feedback-log.md` ENG-267.
```

---

## Summary

| | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Diagnosis** | Five architectural homepage changes in one session, zero documentation steps completed after any of them |
| **Root cause** | EAP-027 + EAP-032: documentation is a post-step competing with the next user message; it is a behavioral rule, not a mechanical pre-condition. 11th occurrence of the same failure mode |
| **Why it's chronic** | The fix has been "more documentation" every time. §8.2 of `engineering.md` says promote to hard gate after 3 recurrences. At 11 recurrences, the escalation is 8 cycles overdue |
| **Fix** | Three-layer: (1) Two-gate stub protocol in AGENTS.md guardrail-engineering-1 — stub before first code change, completion before final response; (2) `engineering.md` §8.4 architectural-change documentation scope table; (3) `docs/design/homepage.md` stale deprecation marker |
| **Anti-patterns cited** | **EAP-027** (Documentation Skips During Rapid-Fire Debugging), **EAP-032** (Architectural Changes Without engineering.md Update) |
| **Principle** | A behavioral rule that competes with urgency will always lose to urgency. The only winning move is to make documentation structurally prior to the action it gates, not structurally after it |
